  "findings": [
    "Intermittent motor circuit issue reported but cannot be verified",
    "Lack of trend logs, load readings, or repeat tests to isolate the failure mode",
    "Symptom cannot be distinguished from normal cycling based on current evidence"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a power quality analyzer or data logger to capture voltage and current trends",
    "Perform a thermal imaging scan of the disconnect switch and motor terminations under load",
    "Conduct insulation resistance testing (megger) on the motor windings and feeder conductors",
    "Review control circuit sequence of operation and check for loose control wiring connections"
  ],
  "rationale": "The reported intermittent failure cannot be diagnosed or verified because the observation began after the symptom stopped, and there is no supporting diagnostic data (such as trend logs or load readings) to differentiate a true fault from normal motor cycling.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Article 430"
  ]
}
